identificationModule.briefTitle: Diastology-Guided Management of Decompensated Heart Failure
identificationModule.officialTitle: DiastologY-Guided MaNAgement of DecoMpensated Heart Failure: A Pilot RandomIzed, Controlled Trial

descriptionModule.briefSummary: More than 30% of people hospitalized with decompensated heart failure return to the hospital within 90 days. Emerging evidence suggests that hemodynamic monitoring with guided management may enhance prognosis and management. Hemodynamic monitoring with echocardiography using diastology and an evidence-informed therapeutic protocol have the potential to achieve this aim in a minimally invasive manner.

armGroups.label: Diastology-Guided Management
armGroups.description: Participants in this arm will undergo a focused echocardiogram every 24 hours. The degree of diastolic dysfunction will be reported to the healthcare team. If the degree of diastolic dysfunction is greater than or equal to grade 2, the research team will recommend escalating the diuretic regimen if either (1) urine output is less than 4,000 mL over 24 hours or (2) weight loss is less than 2 kg over 24 hours. If the degree of diastolic dysfunction is grade 1, the research team will recommend transitioning to or discontinuing oral diuresis.

armGroups.label: Usual Care
armGroups.description: Participants in this arm will also undergo a focused echocardiogram every 24 hours. However, the results of these echocardiograms will not be available for clinical decision-making. Instead, participants will receive usual care by the healthcare team without daily hemodynamic parameters from a study echocardiogram.

interventions.name: Diastology
interventions.description: We will follow American Society of Echocardiography criteria to determine the degree of diastolic dysfunction after performing each focused echocardiogram.

interventions.name: Diastology-Guided Diuresis
interventions.description: If the daily urine output or weight loss are less than study-specified targets, then the research team will recommend changes to the diuretic regimen based on the degree of diastolic dysfunction.

interventions.name: Usual Care
interventions.description: Clinicians will rely on usual care metrics and parameters to guide diuresis.

primaryOutcomes.description: This is calculated as the number of participants enrolled in the study per month.

secondaryOutcomes.description: This is calculated as the number of echocardiograms completed divided by the number scheduled as per the study protocol.
This is calculated as the number of studies without indeterminate diastolic dysfunction divided by the total number of studies performed.

This is calculated as the number of treatment decisions in the intervention arm that is aligned (increase, maintain, or decrease) with the therapeutic recommendations of the trial based on the severity of diastolic dysfunction divided by the number of studies completed.
This is calculated as the number of participants that complete a follow-up visit with a study team member at 30 and 90 days, respectively.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Age ≥18 years at the time of enrolment
2. Decompensated heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (≤40%)
3. Sinus rhythm without conduction abnormalities (second-degree Mobitz II, third-degree, permanent pacemaker)
4. Written informed consent from either the patient or a substitute decision maker

Exclusion Criteria:

1. Mitral annular calcification (at least moderate or severe)
2. Moderate or severe aortic or mitral valvular heart disease, or history of prosthetic mitral valve
3. Atrial fibrillation or flutter
4. History of non-diagnostic echocardiogram
5. Hemodialysis
